---
name: Bug Report
about: Report a typo, formatting issue, broken link, or other minor defect
title: "[Bug] "
labels: bug
assignees: BMBOMICH
---

<!-- ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE

1. Keep the "[Bug]" prefix in the title above and add a short summary
   after it (example: "[Bug] Typo in PSVL.txt line 1234").
2. Under each ## heading below, TYPE YOUR ANSWER on the lines
   provided (or directly above the next heading).
3. Lines wrapped in HTML comment syntax are hidden instructions for
   you; they do NOT appear in the published issue. Leave them as-is or
   delete them, your choice.
4. Sections marked "(optional)" can be left empty.
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

## Have you searched existing issues?

<!-- Check the box by replacing [ ] with [x] -->

- [ ] I searched [**open**](https://github.com/BMBOMICH/psvl/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3Abug)
  and [**closed**](https://github.com/BMBOMICH/psvl/issues?q=state%3Aclosed%20label%3Abug)
  bug reports and this one is not a duplicate

## File and location

<!-- Type the file and line/section where the bug is.
     Example: "PSVL.txt line 1234" or "README.md Quick Comparison table" -->

## What's wrong?

<!-- Type a clear description of the issue. Quote the exact wording if helpful. -->

## What should it be?

<!-- Type the corrected wording or expected behavior. -->

## Screenshots / context (optional)

<!-- Drag-and-drop screenshots here, or add any other helpful context. -->
